description_long: This sconce features a central vertical rod with a decorative, segmented design, suspended from a matching ceiling mount. Radiating from the central axis are six evenly spaced arms, each supporting a spherical, frosted glass globe that likely houses a light bulb. The globes are arranged in a circular pattern, creating a balanced and symmetrical appearance. The fixture is predominantly a light, metallic color, possibly brushed nickel or chrome, with the globes providing a soft, diffused light. The overall design is sleek and modern, with a hint of mid-century influence due to the globe shapes and clean lines. There are no visible switches or chains, suggesting the fixture is hardwired and controlled remotely or by a wall switch.
description: A modern ceiling-mounted sconce with six frosted glass globes on metallic arms, arranged in a symmetrical circular pattern. The fixture has a sleek, decorative central rod and a minimalist, contemporary style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical arrangement of six globes and the decorative central rod.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and even spacing of the globes around the central axis.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, emphasizing the uniformity and circular layout of the globes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with clear visibility of the globes' alignment and central rod.
attribute_fixture_design: The fixture has a modern, minimalist design with mid-century influences, featuring clean lines, spherical glass globes, and a decorative central rod. The overall look is sleek and contemporary, suitable for upscale urban interiors.
